title: Web Server Information Disclosure (phpinfo, phpMyAdmin, Directory Indexing)
description: Apache 2.2.8 with PHP 5.2.4 exposes sensitive information through multiple vectors: phpinfo.php reveals full system configuration, phpMyAdmin is publicly accessible, directory indexing is enabled on /doc/, /test/, /icons/, and HTTP TRACE method is active (XST risk).
evidence:
Web Enumeration Results:

Technologies: Apache/2.2.8 (Ubuntu) DAV/2, PHP/5.2.4-2ubuntu5.10

Exposed endpoints:
- /phpinfo.php - Full PHP configuration disclosure
- /phpMyAdmin/ - Database management interface (public)
- /dav - WebDAV enabled
- /doc/ - Directory indexing (may expose /usr/doc)
- /test/ - Directory indexing
- /server-status - Apache status page

Missing security headers: X-Frame-Options, X-XSS-Protection, X-Content-Type-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, Content-Security-Policy

HTTP TRACE method active (XST vulnerability)
remediation: Remove phpinfo.php and restrict phpMyAdmin access. Disable directory indexing with 'Options -Indexes'. Disable TRACE method. Add security headers.

suggestions: Add command output demonstrating access or data extraction
